payload.name: The Chaotic Caves
hooks: Treasure recovery and monster exterminators are needed because monster attacks near the frontier town have recently increased.
Locals offer rumors of monster activity in the wilderness, including orcs, goblins, gnolls, lizard men, bandits, ghouls near old crypts, and possible spell-casting attackers.
The heroes can explore the marked wilderness sites, recover loot from monsters, rescue captives, and root out the caves threatening nearby towns and villages.

town.name: Town
town.description: An unnamed frontier town on the border between wilderness and civilized lands, beside a river and bridge and protected by a wooden stockade being replaced by a stone wall. It serves as the local home base, with merchants, taverns, craftsmen, church, healer, garrison, militia, docks, warehouse, and many residences.

areas.name: Front Door
areas.description: A large, round green front door to the halfling burrow. It is locked, but old and rotten, and can be easily knocked in.

features.description: locked but rotten and easy to knock in

areas.name: Living Room
areas.description: The main living room. A fireplace is set in the south wall and a round window about 4 feet in diameter is in the north wall. There is no furniture. The stink of death fills the room. A long-dead mauled body in rotten leather armor lies in the center; it carries a silver dagger in a belt sheath, 2 vials of holy water, 1 Potion of Healing, and a leather pouch with 3 cp. A tentacle worm lurks in the dark and scuttles to sounds in adjacent rooms, such as the front door being knocked in.

features.description: small secret niche in fireplace found by searching for loose stones
long-dead mauled body in center of room

areas.name: Dining Room
areas.description: A 15 x 17 foot dining room with two round windows in the north wall. The color of the floor and shapes in the dust indicate that tables and chairs were once placed in the light of the windows.

features.description: two round windows in north wall
dust marks showing where tables and chairs once stood

areas.name: Kitchen
areas.description: A kitchen with a stone stove against the north wall and a chimney going out the roof with a flue about 1 foot square. There are no other furnishings. A round window about 4 feet in diameter is in the northwest corner; the glass is broken out. A pool of slimy green water runs down the sill of the window to the floor. It looks exactly like a green slime monster but is not one.

features.description: round window in northwest corner with broken glass
pool of slimy green water that resembles green slime but is harmless

areas.name: Bedroom
areas.description: An empty bedroom with no furniture. It has a round window about 4 feet in diameter in the south wall.

features.description: round window about 4 feet in diameter in south wall

areas.name: Bedroom
areas.description: An empty bedroom with no furniture. It has a round window about 4 feet in diameter in the south wall.

features.description: round window about 4 feet in diameter in south wall

areas.name: Bedroom
areas.description: An empty bedroom with no furniture. It has a round window about 4 feet in diameter in the southwest corner.

features.description: round window about 4 feet in diameter in southwest corner

areas.name: Back Door
areas.description: A simple round back door about 5 feet high with old, peeling red paint. It is barred from the inside, old, and easily knocked in.

features.description: simple round door about 5 feet high
old and easy to knock in

areas.name: Tree House
areas.description: An old tree house occupied by fire beetles. They will scuttle or jump down to attack. The tree house is a simple square about 20 feet up from the ground and perhaps 6 feet from floor to ceiling.

areas.name: Stables and Storage
areas.description: A shed north of the house entered by double doors into another mound. The floor is dirt, the wall frames are heavy stone blocks, and the ceiling is made of heavy wooden timbers. Round windows about 3 feet in diameter are in the east, north, and west walls; these have shutters but no glass. The north window is broken and smeared with splattered feces. Two empty pony stalls stand in the west end. The east end once held carts and storage. Several piles of what were once sacks of grain lie moldering, covered in a harmless yellow fungus. The dark, humid floor is covered in stinking feces. Stirges fly in and out through the broken north window.

features.description: round shuttered windows in east, north, and west walls
broken north window smeared with feces
two empty pony stalls in west end
moldering grain sacks covered in harmless yellow fungus

areas.name: Buried House
areas.description: A mound of earth unlike the other buildings, overgrown with brush and weeds. Square stones resting in loose earth near the main entrance hint that something is buried here. Digging into the mound for an hour or two reaches the buried house. Searching on top reveals the small one-foot-square chimney to the fireplace in H12. All windows and doors are buried. The two main doors are boarded shut with a great deal of lumber, apparently out of fear.

features.description: earth mound overgrown with brush and weeds
square stones near main entrance hint at burial
digging for an hour or two reaches the house
small one-foot-square chimney visible on top leading to H12 fireplace
two main doors heavily boarded shut

areas.name: Living Room
areas.description: An unfurnished living room with a brick fireplace in the northwest corner. Four humanoid shapes swaddled in cloth lie on the floor; they are zombies that rise and attack if anyone enters the home.

features.description: four cloth-swaddled humanoid shapes on floor

areas.name: Kitchen
areas.description: A kitchen crammed with two cozy chairs, a couch, a table, six wooden chairs, cupboards, a stove, and other furnishings.

areas.name: Master Bedroom
areas.description: The master bedroom. Besides a bed and dresser, it features a writing table. Sitting behind it is a figure in tattered clothes, with exposed features putrid and ragged from wounds and age. This zombie attacks intruders with a flail (as war hammer), growling in hatred of anything living. On the desk are several sheets of old paper scrawled with many different and poorly composed poems to "Kylenne My Lady Love" mixed with the name "Tyrok the Indefatigable." The writer was obviously very disturbed and quite evil. The zombie has a massive hole in its back as if it died from a particularly brutal sword wound.

features.description: old papers with poems to "Kylenne My Lady Love" and the name "Tyrok the Indefatigable"
zombie's back has a massive hole from a brutal sword wound

areas.name: Guest Room
areas.description: A guest room with a bed, table, small dresser, and a few other furnishings still present.

areas.name: Entranceway
areas.description: The cave entrance shows signs of occupation. Bones litter the grassy area outside, and large ogre-sized footprints can be found in muddy or soft ground nearby. Deep enough inside to avoid sunlight, five thin cords are strung across the floor about 2 feet apart and 1–2 inches above the ground, covered by dust and hard to detect without close inspection.

features.description: Large ogre-sized footprints near the entrance
Five dust-covered trip cords across the floor
Broad spiked frame concealed against the east wall

effect.manual: If the cords are tripped, they release a broad frame pulled back against the east wall. It is 8 feet high and wide, lined with sharp wooden spikes, strikes as a 2 HD monster, and deals 2d6 damage on a hit. Some spikes are covered in fresh blood and fur.

areas.name: Ogre's Lair
areas.description: An ogre is here, munching on the corpse of a bear. It may be bribed into helping the party if offered at least 200 gp, but it is incredibly dense, self-interested, easily bored, and may attack intruders or lead allies in circles. It rarely stays with even an agreeable party for more than 1d6 days. The lair is furnished with hides, piles of leaves, and a few tree trunks used as benches and similar furniture. The ogre carries a large knobby club and a crude hide pouch on its belt.

features.description: Hides and piles of leaves used as furnishings
Natural stone steps in the southwest hall descending 10 feet

areas.name: Gray Jelly's Pool
areas.description: Bones litter this wide, 3-foot-deep pool. The ogre sometimes throws body parts into it to watch the gray jelly devour them. A plant-fiber cord is tied to a heavy rock at the pool's edge; the cord has prickly spines along its length to keep the ooze away from it, and a heavy glass bottle is tied to the other end in the pool.

features.description: Plant-fiber retrieval cord with prickly spines

areas.name: Steps
areas.description: Rough natural steps go down sharply about 20 feet in elevation to the north. They are coated in harmless slime.

areas.name: Steps
areas.description: Rough natural steps descend sharply about 20 feet in elevation to the north. Slime and pebbles coat them.

features.description: Slime and pebbles coating the steps

areas.name: Slope Down
areas.description: This passage is a slope descending 30 feet.

areas.name: Stirge Rookery
areas.description: A long, narrow hole in the ceiling winds up about 30 feet to the forest floor above. Around noon, dim light shines down through it. The ground is cluttered with feces and leaves, with worms wriggling in the leaves. Buried under the muck is a wooden chest containing the ogre's valuables from room C2.

features.description: Long narrow ceiling hole to the forest floor 30 feet above
Ground covered in feces and leaves

areas.name: Ornate Room
areas.description: A smashed-in door opens into a room carved out by human hands. It is furnished with a table, chairs, and a single skeletal figure sitting in a chair at the far end of the table. The ogre is deathly afraid of the room and will make an evil-eye sign if anyone gets near it.

features.description: Single skeletal figure seated at the far end of the table

areas.name: Cave-In Trap
areas.description: This section of hallway has a fragile ceiling that can collapse onto intruders.

features.description: Hallway can be blocked by cave-in rubble

effect.manual: Noise and movement from the party cause part of the fragile ceiling to collapse atop the first two or three party members. Each is attacked by a 2 HD monster and takes 1d6 damage if hit; make the attack as a 4 HD monster if they do not have a helmet. The collapse blocks the hallway and requires a half-hour's work to clear.

areas.name: Cave Crickets
areas.description: Fungus and moss cover the floor and walls of this room. Cave locusts prowl here and will jump wildly if spooked.

areas.name: Pool of Water
areas.description: A clear pool of water with a frog, a few small fish, and crayfish fills much of this chamber. Under the mud and muck at the bottom is a bone tube, and there is also a rotten pouch. A rough iron ladder leads up into the ceiling through a tight 3-foot-wide hole and exits in the woods 30 feet overhead.

features.description: Frog, small fish, and crayfish in the pool
Mud and muck at the bottom
Tight 3-foot-wide hole leading to the woods 30 feet above

areas.name: Crypt
areas.description: An old tomb built of heavy stones buried under eons of dirt and brush. A rusted iron gate and door block the entranceway but can be pried open. Worn runes over the doorway in Common read slogans such as "EVIL WILL ALWAYS PREVAIL" and "DESTRUCTION TO ALL THAT IS GOOD." The tomb is a simple rectangle, 10 feet high, 20 feet wide, and 30 feet long from the door to the back wall. Inside are three stone coffins (sarcophagi). The main occupant rests in the middle sarcophagus and avoids daylight, remaining inside until darkness falls.

features.description: rusted iron gate and door at the entrance
middle sarcophagus holds the main occupant

areas.name: Entrance
areas.description: Gaping entranceway to the orc lair. Two orcs usually stand guard outside, leaning on spears, while the first chamber inside serves as their usual bunk room. These orcs are brash, haughty, cruel, and tricky like the rest of lair A.

features.description: First chamber used as bunk room

areas.name: Empty Room
areas.description: A ruined chamber whose walls and part of the ceiling collapsed some time ago. It is currently unfit to live in.

areas.name: Common Room
areas.description: Quarters for a number of orcs, with weapons and gear. Crude furniture stands in the center, wooden bunks line the walls, and the back of the room holds sacks of foodstuffs such as potatoes and hanging meat.

features.description: Stored vegetables and meat weighing 12–72 pounds each

areas.name: Wolf Pen
areas.description: A wolf pen with both ends blocked by wooden fences spanning the full width and height of the halls. Each fence has a 3-foot-wide, 6-foot-high wooden gate lashed shut by rope. Inside, three wolves are kept. They are usually alert and will bark and howl loudly if they hear or smell careless intruders, potentially drawing wandering orcs and possibly alerting the jailers in area 5. The soft earthen floor is littered with wooden bowls of water, bones, and offal.

features.description: Wooden fence at both ends of the area
Wooden gates, 3' wide and 6' high, lashed shut with rope
Bones and offal scattered on the floor

areas.name: Door to Jail
areas.description: Quarters of the big, sweaty orc who serves as jail warden and chief torturer, along with two assistants. The key to the jail door is usually hung on a hide cord on the wall. The room contains crude furniture and various simple wooden or stone interrogation devices.

features.description: Jail key hung on a hide cord on the wall

areas.name: Jail Area
areas.description: A locked jail where captives are held. Currently it contains a bedraggled merchant missing a finger, which the orcs sent off with a ransom note. If rescued, he promises the party what he has at home, though the orcs clearly overestimate his wealth.

areas.name: Chief's Room
areas.description: Residence and court of the strong, shrewd orc chief. He sits on a large, heavily built throne when judging court. A sub-room to the south contains his bed and other personal furniture. His pets are a mountain lion and two wolves. The mountain lion wears a collar set with gems and is usually chained by a strong steel chain to a steel hoop at the base of the throne, though the chain can be released quickly. The wolves wear heavy leather collars and are usually chained to the wall beside the door. Usually four orcs are present: two on permanent duty and two serving as messengers and go-fers. The chief carries the key to the west temple door (see area 9). The throne doubles as a treasure chest with a flip-up seat.

features.description: Throne has a flip-up seat and serves as a treasure chest
Southern sub-room with bed and personal furniture
Steel hoop on the base of the throne
Strong steel chain for the mountain lion
Heavy leather collars on the wolves
Chief has the key to the west temple door

areas.name: Common Room/Guard Room
areas.description: A common room and guard post housing several orcs posted against those in lair B. The room is cluttered with crude furniture and common foodstuffs, some bagged and some in the form of dried meats hanging on strings.

areas.name: Pools
areas.description: Clean pools of water fed by underground springs. They provide a year-round source of fresh water and are 6 feet deep, but are otherwise unremarkable.

areas.name: Shared Hallway
areas.description: A hallway shared in relation to the temple. At each end stands a heavy, thick wooden door bound with strong steel bands and fitted with crafty, heavy locks. Each of the two orc chiefs, in rooms 6 and 13, has the key to the opposite side, so they must cooperate to enter the temple. Orc guards on either side had been fighting over the god's favor so savagely that a high priest had the doors locked and the keys distributed to the chiefs with a warning against further misconduct.

features.description: Heavy thick wooden door at each end
Strong steel bands on both doors
Chief in room 6 has the key to the west temple door
Chief in room 13 has the key to the east temple door

areas.name: Orc Temple
areas.description: A well-maintained temple carefully cut from living stone. Four fluted, ornate pillars support the chamber; the floor is tiled with polished green and red marble, and the ceiling rises about 20 feet high. A dais 6 feet above the floor holds a 12-foot-high statue of a vindictive, evil orc god carved from a single piece of shining black basalt. The statue's eyes are red gems. No steps lead onto the dais, as no one is supposed to stand upon it.

features.description: Polished green and red marble floor
12-foot black basalt statue of an evil orc god

areas.name: Entrance
areas.description: The first chamber of the orc lair. Four orcs are immediately inside along with a hobgoblin who joined up with them after looking for greener pastures. Each inhabitant has 1d6 sp.

areas.name: Secret Safe Room
areas.description: A secret safe room known only to the orc chief in room 13. The chamber contains a sword, a pouch with 25 gp, food, flint and steel, a skin of water, a cot, and a cloak. Both secret doors leading into it are exceptionally well hidden, with only a low chance of discovery even on close scrutiny.

effect.manual: Each of the two secret doors is trapped in two ways: opening one without disarming it releases sleeping gas affecting anyone within 10 feet (save vs. Poison or fall asleep for 1d6 hours) and also triggers a loud alarm that rings for 1 minute in the orc chief's quarters.

areas.name: Guard Post
areas.description: Two orcs with spears and hand axes stand guard at this door. The short corridor between this post and the next room provides a buffer zone in case of attack. Each orc has 1d6 sp.

areas.name: Chief's Quarters
areas.description: The wily orc chief resides here, usually with a war leader and two common orcs. The chief carries the key to the eastern temple door at room 9 on a necklace of long pencil-thin gold bars worth 100 gp. The room contains stout wooden furniture. Under a heavy chair in the northeast corner is the alarm bell that rings if either trapped secret door in room 11 is opened without being disarmed first.

features.description: Alarm bell under a heavy chair in the northeast corner
Key to the eastern temple door at room 9

valuables.name: Necklace of long pencil-thin gold bars

effect.manual: A locked chest contains a gas trap. When opened without inserting a long pencil-sized object into a hole in the back, it releases gas in a 10-foot area; those affected must save vs. Poison or die (or suffer a similarly nasty effect at the GM's discretion).

areas.name: Common Room
areas.description: A trash-littered common room housing nine orcs. They are currently throwing rocks at one standing against a wall while several others are bruised; the game is to see who can take the most beating before flinching. Each has 1d6 sp.

areas.name: Common Room
areas.description: A common room housing seven orcs. It is cluttered with miscellaneous debris they have torn up and discarded, along with firewood and stone circles for cooking. Hidden in a hole in the northeast corner, covered by a stack of fireplace rocks and a foot of earth, is a bag of coins.

features.description: Hidden hole in the northeast corner covered by fireplace rocks and a foot of earth

areas.name: Entrance
areas.description: Twin entrances lead into the first chamber, where five hobgoblins are bunked on guard duty. At "A" is a wide underground stream used as a moat. Wooden planks about 15' long used to cross it are stored on the opposite side, and a loud ox horn hangs on a peg to summon someone to come put the planks across; the hobgoblins in area 17 are tasked with that job. If intruders spend too long debating or over-elaborating a crossing plan, roll 1d6 and on a 5 or 6 two hobgoblins from area 17 appear to interrupt. In the northwest corner at "C" is a crude hide painted to resemble stone, used for ambushes by hiding behind it and letting people enter before rushing out.

features.description: Wide underground stream at "A" used as a moat
Wooden crossing planks stored on the opposite side
Ox horn on a peg used to summon hobgoblins from area 17 to place planks
Crude stone-painted hide at "C" used as an ambush screen

areas.name: Trapped Hallway
areas.description: A 20' high hallway supported by three sets of pillars. The hobgoblins have rigged it as a trap corridor.

features.description: Bell-covered net trap that can alert area 17

effect.manual: Thin wires are strung along the floor between the center two sets of pillars. Anyone tripping the wires is attacked by a heavy stone-headed mallet swinging down from behind the pillars; it attacks as a 2 HD creature and deals 1d8 damage on a hit. A moment later, a net covered in bells drops across the hall between the two pillars. Anyone caught under the net is tangled for 1d6 rounds, and their struggles ring the bells loudly, likely alerting hobgoblins from area 17 below.

areas.name: Common Room
areas.description: Six hobgoblins bunk here. They are not wearing armor but will don it if given time. The room has been fixed up, using what were once platforms for caskets as benches and tables. These hobgoblins are tasked with bringing planks to cross the entrance moat when summoned by horn.

features.description: Former casket platforms repurposed as benches and tables
Three fancy funeral shrouds used as hammocks

areas.name: Store Room
areas.description: Usually this room is locked, though not very well: a thief could pick either door with ease and the doors can be knocked in easily. The chief in area 21 has the key to the doors. It is full of odds and ends and minor plunder, including clothing, sacks of grain and potatoes, hanging dried meats, perhaps some hanging "fresh meat" in the form of slain foes, and mining tools such as picks, chisels, and hammers.

features.description: Usually locked, with two easily picked or forced doors
Hanging dried meats and possibly slain foes used as "fresh meat"
Mining tools: picks, chisels, hammers, etc.

areas.name: Sub-Leader
areas.description: This room has been taken over by a skilled and tough hobgoblin who lives in luxury. On the wall are a short bow and two quivers of arrows, along with other items. He carries the key to this room on a string made of hair on his belt.

features.description: Two quivers of arrows on the wall
Room key carried on a hair string belt-cord

areas.name: Guard Room
areas.description: The hobgoblins here have piled up rubble in the hallway and stockpiled holy water to fend off the ghouls to the west.

features.description: Rubble piled in the hallway as a barricade
Stockpile of six vials of holy water for defense against ghouls to the west

areas.name: Chief's Room
areas.description: This chamber contains the hobgoblin chief and three bodyguards. The chief has the key to the store room at area 18. The room contains a plain bed, chairs, tables, and other furnishings.

areas.name: Ghouls
areas.description: Three ghouls lurk here trying to get through the rubble to the hobgoblins in #20. They are not interested in skeletons or zombies as a possible food source.

areas.name: Skeletons
areas.description: This space contains six skeletons, three on the east and three on the west. They stand like guards stiff at attention and attack intruders on sight. The area is cluttered by broken coffins.

areas.name: Zombies
areas.description: Zombies wait patiently here under orders to attack and destroy any intruders. The area contains empty old and rotten coffins or smashed coffins. Amid the splinters of what appears to have been an ornate cabinet are six intact vials of holy water and 56 gp.

areas.name: Skeletons
areas.description: The skeletons posted here attack on sight. The outward facing walls are lined by crypt spaces, 3 high and 3 wide per 10' section; many are empty. Those with anything in them have the remains of poor people. Hanging out the gaping square of one crypt, in plain sight, is a necklace with gems worth 750 gp; the original owners will be glad to have it recovered as a sentimental heirloom and will pay 1,000 gp for it if tracked down.

features.description: outward-facing walls lined by crypt spaces
occupied crypts contain the remains of poor people

areas.name: Guard Post
areas.description: Guard post at the entrance to the goblin warren. Six goblins hunker here ready to fight intruders. Each carries 1d6 cp. They control a net trap by jerking a rope and will use slings to pelt anyone who steps out to try to undo the net. The warren is dark and dank, with many halls 6' to 8' wide and just as high.

features.description: Rope control for the net trap at the entrance

effect.manual: A rope-triggered net trap covers the marked entrance area. Entangled victims are easily subdued, though if left alone they can free themselves in 1d6 rounds. Anyone walking directly under the net is almost certainly trapped.

areas.name: Common Room
areas.description: Common room whose entrance is covered by crude curtains made from rawhide and cheap cloth. Inside, seven unarmored goblins rest on simple but well-made wooden furniture: stools, tables, cots, and bedding of common furs. Each carries 1d6 cp.

features.description: Crude rawhide and cheap-cloth curtains over the entrance
Wooden stools, tables, cots, and bedding of common furs

areas.name: Trap
areas.description: A concealed pit trap in the goblin lair passage.

effect.manual: A 10-foot-deep pit trap. Victims take 1d6 damage from the fall. Its covering is cleverly made; detecting it by tapping for a hollow floor requires a standard trap-finding check to notice a faint noise.

areas.name: Goblin Priest
areas.description: Room of the old goblin priest, spiritual adviser to the chief and tribe. He is wise but petty and cruel, and misguides others for his own profit. He does not have armor and is not really a cleric, so he cannot use the scroll found here.

areas.name: Common Room/Store Room
areas.description: Common room and store room inhabited by eleven unarmored goblins. Large wicker baskets are stacked along the back wall, filled with wheat grain, flour, vegetables, dried meats, and other items. In niches in the southeast wall stand six beautiful 1-foot-tall statues carved from rare wood. Each goblin carries 1d6 cp.

features.description: Large wicker baskets stacked along the back wall containing food stores

areas.name: Goblin Chief Room
areas.description: Room of the goblin chief. The chief and three other goblins live here. The chief wields a Shortsword +1. The north, south, and east walls are covered by curtains made from a hodgepodge of bed linens, tapestries, and hides. Under an overturned bucket in the northeast corner behind the curtains is one cache; another cache is buried in a dug-out hole in the southeast corner under a very clever covering that is hard to find.

features.description: Curtains covering the north, south, and east walls
Overturned bucket in the northeast corner
Hidden dug-out cache in the southeast corner with a clever covering, found like a secret door
Simple wooden box buried in the southeast cache

areas.name: East Entrance
areas.description: East entrance to the bandit lair. Four humans and a dwarf stand guard here; on hot days they take cover just inside the cave entrance. They each have a crossbow and a case of bolts traded out as the guard changes. At night, a dozen torches are kept just inside the entrance along with a covered bowl of hot coals. Each guard carries 1d6+6 sp.

features.description: Dozen torches kept just inside at night
Crossbows and bolt cases for the guards

areas.name: Common Room
areas.description: Common room where six human fighters are bunked. The furniture is light and easy to break down and move, but not very sturdy. The room is lit by two common oil lamps, with oil brought in using a wooden cup as needed. Each bandit carries 1d6+6 sp.

features.description: Wooden cup used to bring oil

areas.name: Locked Store Room
areas.description: A heavy locked storeroom; knocking the door in takes some time. The deputy in room 41 usually has the key. It contains provisions and loot from raids.

features.description: Large clay jug holding eight gallons of lantern oil

areas.name: Common Room
areas.description: Common room occupied by six bandits. The room is lit by two common oil lamps, with oil brought in using a wooden cup as needed. Each bandit carries 1d6+6 sp.

features.description: Wooden cup used to bring oil

areas.name: Leader's Room
areas.description: The bandit leader's room. The leader and two main cronies sit at a table studying a map of the general area with notes about the best prospects for ambushing traders. The leader wields a longsword +1, wears chainmail +1, carries a shield, has the key to room 40, and wears a ring of keys for the dungeon doors in room 38; the key to a locked strongbox is stuffed in one boot. A lantern hangs from a swinging hook; it contains a continual light spell and is kept fueled in case the spell is dispelled or countered by continual darkness. There is also a gallon of lantern oil in a wooden jar.

features.description: Table with map of the general area and ambush notes
Continual light spell inside the lantern
Wooden jar with a gallon of lantern oil
Key to treasure room #40 carried by the captain
Ring of keys to the dungeon doors at #38 on the captain's belt

areas.name: Stables
areas.description: Stables where the bandits keep their animals hidden, taking them out to browse during the day. The dotted lines on the map are wooden fences, with gates where needed. In one corner are a wagon, two saddles and bridles, and two sets of saddlebags; the wagon also holds several large bags of grain, buckets, and other items.

areas.name: Dungeon
areas.description: Dungeon used as a holding area for captives and anyone the captain wants thrown into irons. The captain in room 36 currently has the keys. Cell a holds three merchant prisoners; if rescued and returned to town, one offers a necklace with gems worth 100 gp and the other two offer 100 gp each. Cell b is empty. Cell c holds a recalcitrant bandit imprisoned for pilfering and bad-mouthing everyone; she will try to ingratiate herself with rescuers, posing as a merchant guard, then steal what she can and run. She has no armor and no weapon. Cell d contains three jars of good wine, two jars of cheap wine, and a dozen bottles of ale in a box. Cell e is empty.

features.description: Dungeon doors locked; keys held by captain in room 36

areas.name: Guard Post
areas.description: Guard post where three men are always stationed to guard the main loot, under strict instructions about who to admit. A common oil lamp provides light. Each guard carries 1d6+6 sp.

features.description: Guard post for the main loot

areas.name: Treasure Room
areas.description: Treasure room holding the best loot, secured behind a strong locked door. The bandit captain in room 36 keeps the key.

areas.name: Bandit Lieutenant
areas.description: Quarters shared by the bandit leader's deputy and cronies, though the cronies are usually with the leader in room 36. The lieutenant is an Elven Thief wearing leather armor and carrying a dagger and shortsword; a shortbow and quiver of arrows hang on a peg by the door. The main room has tables, couches, furnishings, and items for normal living. The three back rooms (41a, 41b, 41c) contain beds and other personal items. All furniture is crude and handmade from local materials. Each room has a plain oil lamp, and the main room has a wooden jug with a quart of oil.

features.description: Shortbow and quiver of arrows on a peg by the door
Main room with tables, couches, and living furnishings
Three back rooms (41a, 41b, 41c) with beds and personal items
Plain oil lamp in each room
Wooden jug with a quart of oil in the main room
Deputy usually has the key to room 34

areas.name: West Entrance
areas.description: West entrance to the bandit lair. Four humans and a halfling stand guard here; on hot days they are usually inside the cave entrance. They have crossbows and a case of bolts traded out as the guard changes. A dozen torches are kept just inside the entrance along with a covered bowl of hot coals. Each guard has 1d6+6 sp on his or her person.

features.description: Dozen torches kept just inside the entrance
Crossbows and bolt cases for the guards

areas.name: Guard Post
areas.description: Nine kobolds hunker nervously here. South of this room is a locked storeroom; the chief in room 48 has the key. The storeroom contains dried meat, pickled vegetables in clay jars, and other supplies.

features.description: Storeroom contains dried meat, pickled vegetables in clay jars, and other supplies

areas.name: Guards
areas.description: Seven kobolds lounge on benches and chairs around tables. None have armor.

areas.name: Common Room
areas.description: The doors to this room are made of flimsy wooden poles lashed together; they can be kicked in or heard through easily. The kobolds inside have no armor. Some keep normal rats as pets, though they sometimes eat them.

features.description: Doors can be kicked in easily
Doors can be heard through easily

areas.name: Common Room
areas.description: The door to this room is rickety and easily kicked in or listened through. The kobolds inside do not have armor. Ordinary rats and mice scurry among the litter; some are being cooked on a fire.

features.description: Door can be kicked in easily
Door can be listened through easily
Fire with rats and mice being cooked

areas.name: Trophy Room
areas.description: This open chamber is decorated with old metal helmets, clothing hung on wooden frames like scarecrows, and other mementos of opponents defeated in battle. Most are unimpressive, and some are kobold-sized. A well-hidden secret door in the center of the west wall is 3 feet wide and 6 feet high; it opens by taking a careful grip on one side and pulling. Only the chief knows it is there.

features.description: Clothing hung on wooden frames like scarecrows
Secret door in center of west wall, 3' wide and 6' high
Secret door is well-hidden and opens by pulling one side

areas.name: Chief's Room
areas.description: The kobold chief, two bodyguards, and four normal kobolds live here. The chief has the key to the storeroom at area 43.

features.description: Large clay urn in back with wooden lid tied on by rough fiber rope
Chief holds the key to the storeroom in area 43

areas.name: Escape Route
areas.description: As shown on the map, a pit trap with stakes lies about 10 or 20 feet from the entranceway, covered by a fairly good false floor, though a careful search has a high chance of finding it. The outside exit lies in the middle of the woods and is concealed by living brush and debris, making it hard to find. Seven giant centipedes scuttle in the brush just outside the entrance and attack anyone who comes near.

features.description: Concealed outside exit in the woods
Exit hidden by living brush and debris

effect.manual: Pit trap with stakes about 10' or 20' from the entranceway; covered by a pretty good false floor; inflicts 2d6 damage.

areas.name: North Entrance
areas.description: North entrance to the lizard man lair. Two totem poles flank the rough opening, each capped with a bleached bear skull and feathers. The tunnel slopes sharply downward from the entrance to the first chamber, dropping about 30 feet in elevation. Several boulders here can be rolled into the hallway and packed with mud to prevent flooding.

features.description: two totem poles capped with bear skulls and feathers
boulders that can be rolled into the hallway and packed with mud to prevent flooding

areas.name: Empty Chamber
areas.description: An empty chamber stacked with firewood, dry grass for tinder, and rocks.

areas.name: Common Room
areas.description: Common room serving as the residence of three male lizard men; they are unarmored.

areas.name: Larder (Food Storage)
areas.description: Larder and food storage made up of two chambers separated from each other and from the hallway by reed walls about 3 feet high. The western pool is 2 feet deep and contains fish, eels, crawdads, and similar creatures; the other chamber contains turtles, lizards, and rabbits.

features.description: two storage chambers separated by 3-foot-high reed walls
food animals including fish, eels, crawdads, turtles, lizards, and rabbits

areas.name: Main Harem
areas.description: Main harem and residence of the lizard man leader with two prime female mates and six young. Other than the leader, all are unarmored. The leader wears armor of tough hide and bone and has a 100 gp gem on a thong around his neck marking his dominance instead of the usual 25 gp tribal gem. A leather sack with a drawstring is hidden in a mud-covered hole in the center of the north wall. Two hollow tree trunks rise through the roof and exit above for ventilation; if cornered, the lizard men can dig out through the ceiling in about an hour or less to escape. A fire smolders in one corner smoking several large rats. Smoked meats, vegetables, large hard-baked clay pots and jars, hides and furs, firewood, grasses, and leaves for bedding and fires are piled here. A reed whistle hangs casually on a peg on the wall; it is a Whistle of Summoning Animals.

features.description: hidden hole in the center of the north wall covered with mud
two hollow tree trunks through the roof providing ventilation
ceiling can be dug out for escape in about an hour or less
smoldering fire with large rats smoking over it
large hard-baked clay pots and jars
firewood, grasses, and leaves for bedding and fires

areas.name: Common Room
areas.description: Common room where three male lizard men are busy readying simple bamboo gigs for a fish-and-frog hunt.

features.description: simple bamboo gigs for fish-and-frog hunting

areas.name: Guard Post
areas.description: Guard post where three armored lizard men and a giant gecko lizard on a thick fiber rope lurk in the dark to keep intruders, including animals, out. The tunnel slopes sharply down from the opening to here, dropping about 30 feet. Several boulders here can be rolled into the hallway and packed with mud to prevent flooding.

features.description: sloping tunnel descending about 30 feet from the entrance
boulders that can be rolled into the hallway and packed with mud to prevent flooding
thick fiber rope for the giant gecko lizard

areas.name: South Entrance
areas.description: South entrance opening into the bank of the stream, which is about 20 feet high here. It is flanked by two 9-foot totem poles featuring crude animals and lizards, capped by bear skulls and feathers.

features.description: entrance opens into a stream bank about 20 feet high
two 9-foot totem poles with crude animals and lizards
bear skulls and feathers atop the totem poles

areas.name: Entrance
areas.description: Entrance to the bugbear lair. Two bugbears with pole arms stand outside the entranceway, one on each side, and another waits in the niche just inside. Heavy wooden beams support the ceilings of the lair; most ceilings are 15 feet high.

areas.name: Swinging Arm Trap
areas.description: Both ends of this hall are protected by the same type of mechanism: a swinging frame set with sharp, hard wooden spikes that slashes out when a pressure plate is stepped on. Each person in front might be hit.

effect.manual: Pressure plates at both ends trigger a swinging spiked wooden frame that attacks as a 2 HD monster and deals 2d6 damage to each person hit.

areas.name: Store Room
areas.description: The door to this room is open. Just inside hangs a tarp meant to prevent light from getting in or out. The west side of the tarp has been carefully cultured with yellow mold so that anyone pushing brusquely through it will release spores. The bugbears normally use a pole to lift the tarp aside from a distance. The room contains crates of food, bags of meat, three 50-foot sections of rope, a dozen iron spikes, and other items.

features.description: Hanging tarp just inside the doorway

effect.manual: A hanging tarp just inside the open door is cultured with yellow mold on its west side; pushing through carelessly releases spores affecting all within 10 feet.

areas.name: Common Room
areas.description: Three bugbears reside here. The east wall is decorated with three long strips of cheap paper covered in bugbear "chicken-tracks" writing: prayer scrolls pleading for success in battle and many enemies to feast on.

features.description: Prayer scrolls on the east wall
Three long strips of cheap paper with bugbear writing

areas.name: Empty Common Room
areas.description: This room has benches and tables stacked on the north wall and bunks and boxes on the south wall. It is empty.

features.description: Benches stacked on the north wall
Tables stacked on the north wall

areas.name: Secret Door
areas.description: This secret door is well hidden. It opens only when a block in the ceiling overhead is gripped and pulled down; the door swings up, and the latch is reset when it is swung down. The prison and main treasure room are hidden beyond to foil rescuers or plunderers. Captives are blindfolded before entering or exiting this area to keep it secret.

areas.name: Treasure Room
areas.description: The door to this room is extremely strong and locked with a high-quality lock; the chief in room 68 has the key. Chests, boxes, and barrels fill the room. A small niche is tucked away on one side of the door frame; unless a blade or similar object is inserted to press the hidden latch, a bladder over the door releases gas into the area outside the door.

features.description: Small niche hidden in the door frame

effect.manual: A hidden latch in a small niche beside the door frame must be pressed to prevent a gas bladder over the door from releasing gas outside the room; if open flame is nearby, the gas ignites and deals 1d6 damage to all within 10 feet (save vs. Dragon Breath for half). Without flame it is harmless and dissipates in 1d6 turns.

areas.name: Prison
areas.description: The prison contains cells a through d and torture room e. The head jailer is usually in and around e with an assistant. The heavy wooden doors to cells a to d are locked and have barred windows for inspection. Area e has stools for guards, iron loops and chains on the walls for victims, and a hot brazier with fireplace poker ready at all times. Cells a and d are empty. Cell b holds a desperate merchant who will pay 200 gp in thanks if returned to town. Cell c contains two completely harmless skeletons of humans or elves.

features.description: Iron loops and chains on the walls
Cell b holds a desperate merchant
Cell c holds two harmless skeletons

areas.name: Common Room
areas.description: Three bugbears reside here. The room is furnished with common rattan furniture, including tables and chairs, and heavy wooden bunks.

areas.name: Owlbear
areas.description: Listening at this door reveals grunting and snuffling. Inside, a great owlbear is chained to the east wall. It was captured as a cub and raised by the bugbears, considers the lair its home, and defends it ferociously against intruders. The chain is weakly attached to the wall and can easily be ripped out if needed. The bugbears sometimes let it roam free and often take it outside with them.

features.description: Door from which grunting and snuffling can be heard
Weak wall attachment for the chain

areas.name: Main Boss
areas.description: The head bugbear and a small guard of common bugbears reside here among heavy wooden beds, chairs, and tables. The chief wears two keys on a thong around one wrist: one opens the locked door to the west and the other opens the treasure room (64). The locked western door is stout, thick, and iron-bound, but the room beyond it is empty.

features.description: Stout thick iron-bound locked door to the west
Empty room beyond the western door

areas.name: Entrance
areas.description: The entrance appears unguarded. A dead goblin and two dead bugbears lie outside, dead for several days and only nibbled by small animals and birds. The place is quiet, reeks of desolation, and any tracks are days old. This is a setup for an ambush: gnolls from areas 70 and 71 watch from around the corner at location "w." Depending on intruder actions, those from 70 may charge from the west opening or come out through their door, while those from 71 may use a well-concealed secret door to attack from behind.

features.description: Well-concealed secret door used by gnolls from 71

effect.manual: Hidden gnoll ambush coordinated from areas 70 and 71; watchers at location "w" observe intruders, with gnolls from 70 attacking from the west opening or their room door and gnolls from 71 using a concealed secret door to strike from behind.

areas.name: Guard Post
areas.description: A shabby guard post with three crude benches carved from sections of a large tree. One gnoll is posted at location "w" with a gnoll from area 71 and alerts the others here if intruders are detected. Each gnoll carries 1d6 ep.

features.description: Three crude benches carved from large tree sections

areas.name: Guard Post
areas.description: A shabby guard post occupied by three gnolls. One stands watch at location "w" alongside a gnoll from area 70 and alerts the others here if intruders are spotted. Each gnoll carries 1d6 ep.

features.description: Secret door used for flanking attacks

areas.name: Common Room
areas.description: A common room where a group of gnolls lounge, usually bored and bickering. A pet wolf is tied by rope to the northwest corner, fed scraps, and trained as both a guard and war dog; it is likely to hear or smell anyone lingering outside the doors. Each gnoll has 1d6 ep. The unmarked room to the southwest contains crude cots.

features.description: Pet wolf tied by rope to the northwest corner
Unmarked room to the southwest with crude cots

areas.name: Cavern
areas.description: A natural cavern divided by stone columns. It is otherwise empty, though dripping water has formed pools of clean drinking water.

areas.name: Leader's Room
areas.description: The gnoll leader lives here with two mates. The leader fights with a Battle Axe +1 and wears a golden necklace set with gems worth 700 gp. The room is cluttered with junk, including many clay drinking jars, many smashed. One jar contains two keys tied together; they open the treasure room to the south and the storeroom at area 75.

features.description: Collection of clay drinking jars, many smashed

areas.name: Treasure Hoard
areas.description: The gnoll leader keeps a giant black widow spider here, feeding it rats and kobolds and cleaning up after it. The spider lurks overhead in dusty webs about 10 feet up; the real ceiling is 15 feet high. It had a bad experience with the door trap and avoids it. A strong chest and other boxes hold coins and assorted goods.

effect.manual: The door is trapped with a heavy flint axe-head mounted on a strong wooden bar that swings down; it attacks as a 3 HD monster and deals 1d8+2 damage on a hit.

areas.name: Storeroom
areas.description: A locked storeroom crammed with barrels, boxes, and bags. The gnoll leader in area 74 has the key. Most of the contents are food or cheap ale.

features.description: Barrels, boxes, and bags crammed into the room
Stores of food and cheap ale

areas.name: Dungeon
areas.description: A gnoll stands guard here with the key and has 1d6 ep. The area is used to hold captives for ransom, though there are no prisoners at present. The cell doors are thick wooden bars with one-inch gaps, fitted with iron hinges and locks; the locks are poor quality and easy for a thief to pick. Cell "a" is a common cell, cell "b" is for segregated prisoners, and area "c" is reached by climbing rough-cut steps about 10 feet up. Area "c" contains a torture area with a chopping block, hand axe, wooden rack, several wooden sawhorses, a pile of coal beside an iron brazier, and crude iron pokers of various types.

features.description: Thick wooden-bar cell doors with 1-inch gaps
Iron hinges and locks of poor quality
Rough-cut steps leading 10' up to area "c"

areas.name: Gateway Flanked by Towers
areas.description: A corroded brass plaque over the gates reads "Roslof Manor." The old wooden gates were battered down long ago and recently someone removed the stronger timbers and much of the metal hardware, laying the rest out in a pattern as if preparing to replace them. The area has been cleaned for easy access; hoofprints and wagon tracks mark the soft earth among badly broken paving stones.

features.description: Ruined wooden gates with salvaged timbers and metal hardware laid out for replacement

areas.name: West Gate Tower
areas.description: A gate tower originally three stories high plus roof; the top floors have collapsed inward, leaving only the bottom floor. It is a hollow tube with a steep stairway that would have reached each floor, and arrow slits every 10 feet facing outward. The entrance door has a lock but is not kept locked; a heavy bar lies beside it to fit brackets on the frame. This tower serves as a guard post for human fighters in scarlet cloaks, red-enameled helmets, and chain mail. They are currently dealing with a giant black widow spider in the rubble overhead, which may scuttle down the wall to attack.

features.description: Door with lock and nearby heavy bar

areas.name: East Gate Tower
areas.description: Like area 78, but this tower has been badly damaged and is unsafe to use. No humans are stationed here. The door's lock is broken off its hinges and the door leans askew in the frame, wedged in place by heavy wooden pegs. The lower floor is full of rubble occupied by giant centipedes. The rubble of the next floor is overrun with giant rats amid broken furniture and harmless skeletal remains.

features.description: Broken door wedged in frame with heavy wooden pegs

areas.name: Stables
areas.description: Ruined stables left in neglect. The area once included a smithy, small cottages, hay storage, wagon storage, rooms for grooms, and similar structures, but these have collapsed into a maze of darkness and ruin. Soot and burned timbers from ancient fires are everywhere, and weeds grow through the rubbish. A giant weasel is sparring with three giant shrews over a pack of giant rats the shrews killed; if they smell fresh meat they attack it instead.

features.description: Ruined remains of former stables complex
Collapsed structures forming a maze of darkness and ruin
Soot and burned timbers from ancient fires

areas.name: Cottages
areas.description: Former quarters for gardeners and other staff, now ruined much like area 80. Berries, fruit trees, and vegetables grow wild here. During the growing season, characters can gather 1d6 weeks of rations, though most will spoil within a week or two unless preserved.

features.description: Wild berries, fruit trees, and vegetables

areas.name: North Wall
areas.description: This area of rotten wooden buildings and weeds was once used for dog kennels. The wall here is patrolled by the humans' minotaur ally. If it notices intruders, it bellows to attract guards, throws rocks for 1d6 rounds or so, then charges down the nearest steps. In the northeast corner stands a rude lean-to of hides sheltering the half-eaten raw carcass of a deer, with animal parts strewn around.

features.description: Rotten wooden buildings from former dog kennels
Lean-to of hides in the northeast corner
Half-eaten deer carcass and scattered animal parts

areas.name: Moat
areas.description: A 10-foot-deep moat surrounds the main house and is currently full. A simple wooden bridge once crossed it at the front entrance, but it burned down long ago, leaving only footing planks and timbers on each side. The moat is fed by a slow-moving stream entering under the north wall and exiting to the east. Each opening has an arched undercut with two sets of heavy iron bars, one on each side, to keep out intruders.

features.description: Burned-out remains of former wooden bridge
Slow-moving stream entering under the north wall and exiting east
Two sets of heavy iron bars at each opening

areas.name: Main Doors
areas.description: The great iron double doors of the main house are firmly shut and barred from the inside and cannot be opened without something like a massive ram. High wide windows along the walls are barred with heavy iron, preventing entry by anything bigger than a squirrel. Forcing entry through the windows or by other noisy means is likely to attract attention from occupants. On the west side of the door, some blocks can be easily pulled from the wall where earlier invaders made a hole and the current occupants stacked the blocks back sloppily, allowing easy access into room 85. Nearby lie three wooden ramps, each about 24 feet long, made from collected timbers and iron hardware to let wagons cross the moat.

features.description: Great iron double doors barred from inside
High wide windows with heavy iron bars
Loose wall blocks on west side that can be removed to make an entry hole into 85
Three wooden wagon ramps about 24 feet long

areas.name: Main Hall
areas.description: The main hall's lower floor is heavily used, with many hardwood timbers torn out or scuffed and dirt everywhere; the upstairs is cleaner and less damaged. A heavy iron chandelier hangs overhead for lanterns or torches, with smaller wall mounts throughout the manor. Skylights light the hall by day, and most rooms have outside windows. Two ornate stairs marked a and b rise about 20 feet to the second-floor balcony. Under the balcony are swinging double doors leading to the back. The main hall ceiling is about 30 feet high; other rooms are about 20 feet high. Recent bootprints in the dust lead everywhere. Some adjoining-room hardwood walls have been torn down in places. Horses can be heard in areas 86 and 87. As adventurers enter, three fighters in red surcoats over chain mail, carrying swords, are coming down the stairs, discussing whether raiding towns or waylaying travelers would make them richer. One is a woman with a longsword +1. They may try to lure intruders upstairs to area 106 while one fetches troops from room 88. A smith and two assistants from the stables join them if alerted by shouting or battle noise.

features.description: Two ornate stairways (a and b) up to second-floor balcony
Swinging double doors under the balcony

areas.name: Renovated Stables
areas.description: These rooms were once a library, music room, and gymnasium, but have been gutted and fitted with simple wooden stalls to serve as a smithy and stables. There are four riding horses, three mules, and three oxen here. Nearby are hay, feed bags, sacks of grain, and similar supplies. In one corner are a small traveling anvil, a pile of bricks, bags of coal, several hammers, other tools, and a few horseshoes. Two horses were lost to wandering monsters before the rest of the animals were moved inside for safety.

areas.name: Wagon Storage
areas.description: The south end of this area was once a posh sitting room, but the walls were knocked out to make room for wagons and equipment. Two wagons and two carts are stored here along with boxes, tarps, and other gear. The northern end was once a long dining hall. Old smashed tables and chairs are stacked neatly like firewood, and some thicker pieces are arranged like building timbers ready for use. There is ample room to move between the piles to reach the kitchen and prep room at the back.

features.description: Stacks of smashed tables and chairs
Thicker wood pieces arranged like building timbers
Open paths to the kitchen and prep room

areas.name: Prep Room
areas.description: Formerly a prep room buffering the kitchen and dining room, this room now holds tables and chairs, usually strewn with dirty wooden plates, pewter or wooden mugs, and other items. A keg of cheap beer is propped on a table in one corner. A group of men are eating bread, meat, gravy, and cheap beer here. If fighting breaks out, two more men come from the kitchen. The leader wears chain mail and carries a sword; the others wear red-stained leather armor and carry various weapons including short swords, hand axes, and war hammers.

areas.name: Kitchen
areas.description: A kitchen built to prepare large meals, with several ovens and stoves along the outer walls, plus plenty of tables and sinks. Dirty wooden dishes are common. Two men are here cooking stew on one of the stoves; if a fight breaks out they join the men in room 88. They are armed with hand axes and dressed in red-stained leather armor.

areas.name: Store Room
areas.description: A storeroom for dry foods. Bags of flour and other grains and vegetables are stacked here. A narrow stair leads down 10 feet to a cold room where fresh carcasses of venison, boar, rabbits, and other game hang on iron hooks.

features.description: Narrow stairs down 10 feet to cold room
Cold room with game carcasses on iron hooks

areas.name: Laundry Room
areas.description: Formerly a laundry room with buckets and tubs; now only long ropes for hanging laundry remain. Wooden shelves for linens and rugs stand empty. In one corner are two sealed buckets, one containing red paint and the other red varnish.

features.description: Empty wooden shelves for linens and rugs

areas.name: Wash Room
areas.description: A wash room for people, clothes, and dishes. It contains bathtubs, cloth screens on wooden frames, and a table with bars of soap.

areas.name: Back Door
areas.description: The rear exit of the manor. The door is a heavy security door with two iron bars that can be thrown across from the inside.

features.description: Two iron bars securing it from inside

areas.name: Servant Quarters
areas.description: One of the enlarged former servant quarters, with inner partitions torn down. Two men here are resting after a fight with giant rats. They can still fight and will accost intruders as best they can. They are not wearing their leather armor, though they can put it on if given time.

features.description: Former servant quarters with inner partitions torn down

areas.name: Servant Quarters
areas.description: One of the enlarged former servant quarters, now partly occupied by foot troops, most of whom are eating or elsewhere. The inner partitions have been torn down to enlarge the room.

features.description: Former servant quarters with inner partitions torn down

areas.name: Servant Quarters
areas.description: One of the enlarged former servant quarters, now partly occupied by foot troops, most of whom are eating or elsewhere. The inner partitions have been torn down to enlarge the room.

features.description: Former servant quarters with inner partitions torn down

areas.name: Servant Quarters
areas.description: One of the enlarged former servant quarters, now partly occupied by foot troops. As adventurers enter the hall around this room, a man comes out of the door and, unless stopped, ducks back inside and calls for help. Four men occupy this room, sleeping after watch. Their spears and hand axes are nearby. They are not currently wearing their red-stained leather armor, but will put it on if given time. Each carries coin.

features.description: Former servant quarters with inner partitions torn down

areas.name: Stairway
areas.description: Stairway on the manor upper floor with a safety railing. The stairs descend to area #85 on the ground floor. Rooms on this floor have gas lights, windows, torch holders, and 10' ceilings; the stairs rise 20'.

features.description: Stairs descending to area 85 on the ground floor
Upper floor has gas lighting, windows, and torch holders
10' ceilings on this floor; stairs rise 20'

areas.name: Guest Room
areas.description: Guest room serving as the residence of the female fighter and one of the male fighters encountered coming down the stairs in area #85. Furnished with a makeshift bed, table, chairs, and similar basics.

features.description: Locked casket with a crude lock meant only to indicate tampering

areas.name: Guest Room
areas.description: Guest room occupied by one of the three fighters encountered on the stairs in area #85. Furnished with a makeshift bed, table, chairs, and similar items. A sack of valuables is hidden under the bed, guarded by the owner's pet snake, which may immediately grab and pull someone under if they investigate unwisely.

areas.name: Guest Room
areas.description: Guest room now used by one of the clerics from area #106 as a private room. It has makeshift furnishings: bed, table, chairs, and so on. On a chest of drawers, three silver holy symbols are arranged in a worshipful manner around a golden icon of an evil demonic being known for austerity and obedience.

features.description: Religious display on the chest of drawers

areas.name: Guest Room
areas.description: Guest room claimed by one of the clerics from area #106 as a private room. It contains shabby, slapped-together furniture: bed, table, chairs, and such. A painting of horrors and stark terror hangs on one wall; it has little general value, but a church of good nature might pay about 100 gp for it to burn or use educationally. Beneath it hang items taken from a murdered cleric.

features.description: Painting of horrors and stark terror hanging on the wall
Holy symbol hung beneath the painting
Three necklaces hung beneath the painting

areas.name: Guest Room
areas.description: Guest room now used by the magic-user from area #106 as a private room. Furnishings are an ordinary makeshift bed, table, chairs, and such. The beginnings of an alchemical laboratory for making potions are here, but it is incomplete.

features.description: Incomplete alchemical laboratory for making potions

areas.name: Guest Room
areas.description: Guest room used as a storage room for various items kept for future use.

areas.name: Children's Nursery
areas.description: Former children's bedroom and nursery. The double doors to this room hang open. A few old toys and cribs lie scattered about under a layer of dust.

areas.name: Master Room
areas.description: Lavish, very large master room now used as the headquarters and treasury of the band. The door is shut, and voices can be heard talking inside. Three human leaders are leaning over a map on a table, planning to use monsters to carve out their own empire; they attack intruders unless the intruders surrender. Several gaslights fed by natural gas taps illuminate the sparse furnishings: a large round table, chairs, and a desk; the original furnishings were looted long ago. The magic-user's spell book is nearby, and it contains the spells he knows. Two large chests in one corner hold the band's war funds.

features.description: Small warning bell hung on the inside of the door
Several gaslights fed by natural gas taps
Two large chests holding war funds

effect.manual: A small bell hung from a rod on the inside near the top of the door jingles if the door is not opened carefully. The magic-user's spell book cover has a needle trap; save vs. Poison or die.

abilities.name: wearing plate mail and wielding mace and shield
abilities.prose: wearing plate mail and wielding mace and shield

abilities.name: Spells: 1st level – cure light wounds, remove fear
abilities.prose: Spells: 1st level – cure light wounds, remove fear

abilities.name: wearing plate mail and wielding mace and shield
abilities.prose: wearing plate mail and wielding mace and shield

abilities.name: Spells: 1st level – cure light wounds, light
abilities.prose: Spells: 1st level – cure light wounds, light

abilities.name: Magic Items: Potion of Healing, Potion of Invisibility
abilities.prose: Magic Items: Potion of Healing, Potion of Invisibility

abilities.name: Spells: 1st level – sleep, magic missile; 2nd level – detect invisible, web
abilities.prose: Spells: 1st level – sleep, magic missile; 2nd level – detect invisible, web

abilities.name: Magic Items: Wand of Magic Missiles (11 charges), Ring of Protection +1, Cloak of Displacement
abilities.prose: Magic Items: Wand of Magic Missiles (11 charges), Ring of Protection +1, Cloak of Displacement
